title: Week 08 - Array Statistics and Extremes
description: Practice array summation, counting, maximums, minimums, and second-largest values.

questions.title: Calculate the Total Score of a Class Score Sheet

description.background: The teacher stores the first n students' scores in order in the array scores. To summarize the exam, the total score of these students should be calculated first as the basis for later average or score distribution analysis.
description.task: Traverse the first n scores in scores, add each student's score, and return the final total score.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the number of students n.
description.hint: Prepare a total variable starting from 0. Each time you visit a score, add it to total.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local total = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        total = total + scores[i]
    end
    return total
end

questions.title: Calculate the Average Daily Coin Reward

description.background: The game system gives the player coin rewards each day, stored in order in the array coins. The player wants to know the average daily coins earned over the first n days to judge whether the rewards are stable.
description.task: First add up the coin total for the first n days in coins, then divide by n to calculate and return the average daily reward.
description.input: Two parameters: the coin array coins and the number of days n.
description.hint: Use a loop to calculate the coin total first, then return total / n.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, n)
    local total = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        total = total + coins[i]
    end
    return total / n
end

questions.title: Count How Many Students Passed

description.background: The teacher has stored the first n students' exam scores in the array scores. The school rule says that a score of 60 or above is passing. Count how many students in this score sheet reached the passing line.
description.task: Traverse the first n scores in scores, count scores greater than or equal to 60, and return the number of passing students.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the number of students n.
description.output: Return the number of passing students.
description.hint: Use count to record passing students. Whenever scores[i] >= 60, add 1 to count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local count = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 60 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Find the Best Player in a Long Jump Competition

description.background: After a long jump competition, each athlete's result is stored in competition order in the array jumps. A larger long jump result means the athlete jumped farther and ranks higher. The judge needs to find the best athlete among the first n athletes and that athlete's result.
description.task: Traverse the first n results in jumps, find the maximum result and its athlete position, and return the position and result in order.
description.input: Two parameters: the long jump result array jumps and the number of athletes n.
description.output: Return two values: the best athlete position and the best result.
description.hint: Assume athlete 1 is best at first, then compare from athlete 2 onward. If you find a larger result, update both the best result and position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(jumps, n)
    local bestIndex = 1
    local bestValue = jumps[1]
    for i = 2, n do
        if jumps[i] > bestValue then
            bestValue = jumps[i]
            bestIndex = i
        end
    end
    return bestIndex, bestValue
end

questions.title: Find the Lowest Score and Its Position

description.background: When reviewing a score sheet, the teacher wants to know not only the lowest score but also the student position where it appears. The first n student scores are stored in order in scores, and the minimum needs to be found by comparison.
description.task: Traverse the first n scores in scores, find the lowest score and its position, and return the position and lowest score in order.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the number of students n.
description.output: Return two values: the lowest score position and the lowest score.
description.hint: Assume the first score is the lowest at first. Then compare one by one; if a smaller score is found, update both the lowest score and its position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local minIndex = 1
    local minValue = scores[1]
    for i = 2, n do
        if scores[i] < minValue then
            minValue = scores[i]
            minIndex = i
        end
    end
    return minIndex, minValue
end

questions.title: Count Game Scores Above a Target Line

description.background: After a game activity, player scores are stored in order in the array points. The activity sets a target line target, and only scores strictly greater than target are counted as meeting the goal. Count how many of the first n players exceeded the target line.
description.task: Traverse the first n scores in points, count all scores greater than target, and return the number of players above the target line.
description.input: Three parameters: the score array points, the number of players n, and the target line target.
description.output: Return the count of scores greater than target.
description.hint: The problem requires exceeding target, so scores equal to target are not counted. Only increase the count when points[i] > target.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(points, n, target)
    local count = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        if points[i] > target then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Find the Runner-Up Score in a Score Ranking

description.background: After a competition, all athlete scores are stored in the array scores. The highest distinct score on the leaderboard is the champion score, and the second-highest distinct score is the runner-up score. Find the runner-up score among the first n scores.
description.task: First find the highest score, then find the largest score among all scores smaller than the highest score, and return it as the runner-up score.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the number of athletes n.
description.hint: Traverse the array twice: first find maxValue, then find the largest score less than maxValue.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local maxValue = scores[1]
    for i = 2, n do
        if scores[i] > maxValue then
            maxValue = scores[i]
        end
    end
    local second = scores[1]
    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] < maxValue then
            if second == maxValue or scores[i] > second then
                second = scores[i]
            end
        end
    end
    return second
end

questions.title: Find the Difference Between First and Second Place in Long Jump

description.background: In a long jump competition, a larger result means a farther jump. After the competition, all athlete results are stored in the array jumps. The judge wants to know the difference between first place and second place, so the highest result and the second-highest distinct result must be found.
description.task: First find the highest result in jumps, then find the largest result below the highest result, and finally return the highest result minus the second-highest result.
description.input: Two parameters: the long jump array jumps and the number of athletes n.
description.output: Return first place result minus second place result.
description.hint: Reuse the second-largest-value method. First find best, then find the largest value second below best, and finally return best - second.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(jumps, n)
    local best = jumps[1]
    for i = 2, n do
        if jumps[i] > best then
            best = jumps[i]
        end
    end
    local second = jumps[1]
    for i = 1, n do
        if jumps[i] < best then
            if second == best or jumps[i] > second then
                second = jumps[i]
            end
        end
    end
    return best - second
end

questions.title: Count Excellent, Passing, and Needs-Improvement Score Ranges

description.background: The teacher needs to classify students by score range. Scores of 85 or above are excellent, scores from 60 to 84 are passing, and scores below 60 need improvement. The first n student scores are stored in scores.
description.task: Traverse the first n scores in scores, count the excellent, passing, and needs-improvement students separately, and return the three counts in order.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the number of students n.
description.output: Return three values: excellent, passing, and needs-improvement counts.
description.hint: Prepare three count variables: excellent, pass, and improve. Check for >= 85 first, then >= 60, and the remaining scores need improvement.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    local excellent = 0
    local pass = 0
    local improve = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        if scores[i] >= 85 then
            excellent = excellent + 1
        elseif scores[i] >= 60 then
            pass = pass + 1
        else
            improve = improve + 1
        end
    end
    return excellent, pass, improve
end
